title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. ochroleuca
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. ochroleuca, also known as the pale marsh orchid or green-flowered marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid found in marshy areas, particularly in the UK and parts of Europe. Traditionally, the roots of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. ochroleuca have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and antitussive properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. ochroleuca can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
